title: allowWrite:[~/lab-fs-write] -> その1パスだけ cwd 外に穴が開く。他の cwd 外は依然 allow x ng

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > inside.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > $HOME/lab-fs-write/probe.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > $HOME/lab-glob-XYZ/probe.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

notes: 1-variable diff from a: + allowWrite:[~/lab-fs-write]. Write is an allowlist: the default boundary is cwd(+tmp), so allowWrite opens EXACTLY the listed dir. w-allowlisted flips a->b from ng to ok; w-cwd stays ok; w-glob stays ng (a different ~ path, not listed). The exact-path nature is the point -- allowWrite:[~/lab-fs-write] does not open ~/lab-glob-XYZ.
hypothesis: allowWrite:[~/lab-fs-write] opens exactly that directory for sandboxed Bash writes; the write there succeeds (allow x ok) while cwd stays writable and any other outside-cwd path (~/lab-glob-XYZ) is still EPERM (allow x ng).
